title: Pan Seared Uruguayan Shrimp Empanadas
description: A traditional Uruguayan dish featuring shrimp and empanadas, made with a fermented dough and crispy exterior, perfect for a lazy Sunday, and halal friendly. This recipe uses a flambe technique to add a rich flavor to the shrimp.
instructions: Combine flour, salt, and pepper in a large bowl to make the empanada dough.
Gradually add water to the bowl and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Let the dough rest for 30 minutes to allow it to ferment.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the shrimp to the skillet and cook until they are pink and fully cooked.
Add the rum to the skillet and flambe the shrimp for 30 seconds.
Place a tablespoon of the shrimp mixture onto the center of each empanada dough circle.
Brush the edges of the dough with the egg wash and fold the dough in half to form a half moon shape.
Press the edges of the dough together to seal the empanada.
Heat a large skillet with about 1/2 inch of vegetable oil over medium heat and pan fry the empanadas until they are crispy and golden brown.
